title: Tesla Robotaxis Go To Florida - Slashdot
excerpt: Tesla says it is launching its Robotaxi service in Orlando and Tampa, though it has not shared fleet sizes or whether customers can immediately hail rides. The Verge notes the announcement lands on Tesla earnings day and comes as the company's robotaxi rollout remains far smaller than Elon Musk prev...
content_html:
Tesla says it is launching its Robotaxi service in Orlando and Tampa, though it has not shared fleet sizes or whether customers can immediately hail rides. The Verge notes the announcement lands on Tesla earnings day and comes as the company's robotaxi rollout remains far smaller than Elon Musk previously projected, with tracker data showing only a small number of unsupervised vehicles operating in existing cities. From the report: [B]ack in May, Tesla had five unsupervised robotaxis in Dallas, six in Houston, and 29 in Austin. As of today, there are only 17 unsupervised vehicles in Austin, four in Dallas, and zero in Houston, according to the Robotaxi Tracker. By comparison, Waymo is estimated to have over 3,500 fully driverless vehicles in operation across over 10 cities.

Like Waymo, Tesla typically introduces robotaxis to a new city with a safety driver behind the wheel. Unlike Waymo, sometimes Tesla moves that person over to the passenger seat with access to a kill switch should anything go wrong. The company has been inconsistent in how it rolls out its robotaxis to new markets. The numbers go up and down, with zero explanation from the company as to why.

[...] By adding new cities, Tesla is clearly trying to sell investors on the idea that its robotaxi project is growing. But the fluctuating fleet size, inconsistency in supervised vs unsupervised vehicles, and long wait times tell a very different story.
content_text: Tesla says it is launching its Robotaxi service in Orlando and Tampa, though it has not shared fleet sizes or whether customers can immediately hail rides. The Verge notes the announcement lands on Tesla earnings day and comes as the company's robotaxi rollout remains far smaller than Elon Musk previously projected, with tracker data showing only a small number of unsupervised vehicles operating in existing cities. From the report: [B]ack in May, Tesla had five unsupervised robotaxis in Dallas, six in Houston, and 29 in Austin. As of today, there are only 17 unsupervised vehicles in Austin, four in Dallas, and zero in Houston, according to the Robotaxi Tracker. By comparison, Waymo is estimated to have over 3,500 fully driverless vehicles in operation across over 10 cities. Like Waymo, Tesla typically introduces robotaxis to a new city with a safety driver behind the wheel. Unlike Waymo, sometimes Tesla moves that person over to the passenger seat with access to a kill switch should anything go wrong. The company has been inconsistent in how it rolls out its robotaxis to new markets. The numbers go up and down, with zero explanation from the company as to why. [...] By adding new cities, Tesla is clearly trying to sell investors on the idea that its robotaxi project is growing. But the fluctuating fleet size, inconsistency in supervised vs unsupervised vehicles, and long wait times tell a very different story.

blocks.html_en:
Tesla says it is launching its Robotaxi service in Orlando and Tampa, though it has not shared fleet sizes or whether customers can immediately hail rides. The Verge notes the announcement lands on Tesla earnings day and comes as the company's robotaxi rollout remains far smaller than Elon Musk previously projected, with tracker data showing only a small number of unsupervised vehicles operating in existing cities. From the report: [B]ack in May, Tesla had five unsupervised robotaxis in Dallas, six in Houston, and 29 in Austin. As of today, there are only 17 unsupervised vehicles in Austin, four in Dallas, and zero in Houston, according to the Robotaxi Tracker. By comparison, Waymo is estimated to have over 3,500 fully driverless vehicles in operation across over 10 cities.

Like Waymo, Tesla typically introduces robotaxis to a new city with a safety driver behind the wheel. Unlike Waymo, sometimes Tesla moves that person over to the passenger seat with access to a kill switch should anything go wrong. The company has been inconsistent in how it rolls out its robotaxis to new markets. The numbers go up and down, with zero explanation from the company as to why.

[...] By adding new cities, Tesla is clearly trying to sell investors on the idea that its robotaxi project is growing. But the fluctuating fleet size, inconsistency in supervised vs unsupervised vehicles, and long wait times tell a very different story.

sentences.en: Tesla says it is launching its Robotaxi service in Orlando and Tampa, though it has not shared fleet sizes or whether customers can immediately hail rides.
sentences.zh: 特斯拉表示，它将在奥兰多和坦帕推出 Robotaxi 服务，但没有透露车队规模，也没有透露客户是否可以立即叫车。

sentences.en: The Verge notes the announcement lands on Tesla earnings day and comes as the company's robotaxi rollout remains far smaller than Elon Musk previously projected, with tracker data showing only a small number of unsupervised vehicles operating in existing cities.
sentences.zh: The Verge 指出，这一消息是在特斯拉财报日发布的，当时该公司的机器人出租车部署规模仍远小于埃隆·马斯克此前的预期，跟踪数据显示，现有城市中只有少量无人监管的车辆在运营。

sentences.en: From the report: [B]ack in May, Tesla had five unsupervised robotaxis in Dallas, six in Houston, and 29 in Austin.
sentences.zh: 报告摘自：5 月份，特斯拉在达拉斯拥有 5 辆无人监管的机器人出租车，在休斯顿拥有 6 辆，在奥斯汀拥有 29 辆。

sentences.en: As of today, there are only 17 unsupervised vehicles in Austin, four in Dallas, and zero in Houston, according to the Robotaxi Tracker.
sentences.zh: 根据 Robotaxi Tracker 的数据，截至目前，奥斯汀只有 17 辆无人监管车辆，达拉斯有 4 辆，休斯顿有 0 辆。

sentences.en: By comparison, Waymo is estimated to have over 3,500 fully driverless vehicles in operation across over 10 cities.
sentences.zh: 相比之下，Waymo 预计在 10 多个城市运营超过 3,500 辆完全无人驾驶汽车。

sentences.en: Like Waymo, Tesla typically introduces robotaxis to a new city with a safety driver behind the wheel.
sentences.zh: 与 Waymo 一样，特斯拉通常会将机器人出租车引入新城市，并由安全驾驶员负责驾驶。

sentences.en: Unlike Waymo, sometimes Tesla moves that person over to the passenger seat with access to a kill switch should anything go wrong.
sentences.zh: 与 Waymo 不同的是，如果出现任何问题，特斯拉有时会将该人转移到乘客座位上，以便使用终止开关。

sentences.en: The company has been inconsistent in how it rolls out its robotaxis to new markets.
sentences.zh: 该公司在向新市场推出机器人出租车的方式上一直不一致。

sentences.en: The numbers go up and down, with zero explanation from the company as to why. [...]
sentences.zh: 这些数字有起有落，公司对原因的解释为零。 [...]

sentences.en: By adding new cities, Tesla is clearly trying to sell investors on the idea that its robotaxi project is growing.
sentences.zh: 通过增加新的城市，特斯拉显然是想让投资者相信其机器人出租车项目正在增长。

sentences.en: But the fluctuating fleet size, inconsistency in supervised vs unsupervised vehicles, and long wait times tell a very different story.
sentences.zh: 但车队规模的波动、监管车辆与无人监管车辆的不一致以及漫长的等待时间却讲述了一个截然不同的故事。

Unlike Waymo, sometimes Tesla moves that person over to the passenger seat with access to a kill switch should anything go wrong. The company has been inconsistent in how it rolls out its robotaxis to new markets. The numbers go up and down, with zero explanation from the company as to why.

sentences.zh: 这些数字有起有落，公司对原因的解释为零。

sentences.zh: 通过增加新的城市，特斯拉显然是想让投资者相信其机器人出租车项目正在增长。

sentences.en: But the fluctuating fleet size, inconsistency in supervised vs unsupervised vehicles, and long wait times tell a very different story.
sentences.zh: 但车队规模的波动、监管车辆与无人监管车辆的不一致以及漫长的等待时间却讲述了一个截然不同的故事。

content_html_zh: 特斯拉表示，它将在奥兰多和坦帕推出 Robotaxi 服务，但没有透露车队规模，也没有透露客户是否可以立即叫车。 The Verge 指出，这一消息是在特斯拉财报日发布的，当时该公司的机器人出租车部署规模仍远小于埃隆·马斯克此前的预期，跟踪数据显示，现有城市中只有少量无人监管的车辆在运营。 报告摘自：5 月份，特斯拉在达拉斯拥有 5 辆无人监管的机器人出租车，在休斯顿拥有 6 辆，在奥斯汀拥有 29 辆。 根据 Robotaxi Tracker 的数据，截至目前，奥斯汀只有 17 辆无人监管车辆，达拉斯有 4 辆，休斯顿有 0 辆。 相比之下，Waymo 预计在 10 多个城市运营超过 3,500 辆完全无人驾驶汽车。 与 Waymo 一样，特斯拉通常会将机器人出租车引入新城市，并由安全驾驶员负责驾驶。 与 Waymo 不同的是，如果出现任何问题，特斯拉有时会将该人转移到乘客座位上，以便使用终止开关。 该公司在向新市场推出机器人出租车的方式上一直不一致。 这些数字有起有落，公司对原因的解释为零。 [...] 通过增加新的城市，特斯拉显然是想让投资者相信其机器人出租车项目正在增长。 但车队规模的波动、监管车辆与无人监管车辆的不一致以及漫长的等待时间却讲述了一个截然不同的故事。
